name: Discord setup guidelines 📜 - get all channels & categories of a Discord server

parameters.content:
## 🎯 **Goal: Test Your Discord Connection**
This workflow demonstrates advanced Discord bot capabilities:
✓ Fetch ALL channels from your Discord server at once
✓ Automatically separate text channels from categories
✓ Merge channel data with category metadata
✓ Prepare structured data for bulk operations

Perfect for: Building multi-channel bots, server management automation, 
and advanced Discord integrations.

What you'll learn: Channel enumeration, conditional logic, data merging, 
and preparing for bulk message operations.

## ⚙️ Workflow Configuration
### Get Server ID:
  • Enable Developer Mode: Discord Settings → Advanced → ☑ Developer Mode
  • Right-click your server icon → Copy Server ID
  • Example format: 012345678911121314(18-19 digit number)
### Configure "Set - Server ID" Node:
  1. Open the "Set - Server ID" node
  2. Replace the server_id value with YOUR server ID
  3. Keep the field name as "server_id" (used by next node)
### Configure "Get channels list" Node:
  • Credential: Select "Testing" (or your credential name)
  • Guild ID: Uses ={{ $json.server_id }} (from previous node)
  • Limit: 20 (increase if you have more than 20 channels)
  • Returns ALL channel types: Text, Voice, Categories, Forums
### Understanding the Flow:
  📌 "Separate Category & Channel" checks parent_id:
     • Has parent_id = Text Channel → Goes to "Get Channels"
     • No parent_id = Category → Goes to "Get Server's Categories"

  📌 "Merge" combines both paths:
     • Matches channels to their parent categories
     • Creates complete channel + category metadata
     • Output: [{ channel_name, channe_id, category_name, category_id, last_message_id }]
### Test the Workflow:
  1. Click "Execute workflow" (top-right)
  2. Check each node for green success checkmarks
  3. Click "Merge" node → View output to see combined data
  4. You should see channels grouped by category

## 🤔 Troubleshooting Guide
### Error 401 (Invalid Token):
  Problem: Your Bot Token in n8n is wrong
  ✓ Fix: Reset token in Discord Developer Portal → Bot → Reset Token
  ✓ Update your n8n credential with new token
### Error 403 (Missing Access):
  Problem: Bot doesn't have View Channels permission
  ✓ Fix: Server Settings → Roles → Enable "View Channels" for bot role
  ✓ Check channel-specific permissions if needed
### Error 404 (Not Found):
  Problem: Server (ID) is incorrect
  ✓ Fix: Double-check Server (ID) in "Set - Server ID" node
  ✓ Enable Developer Mode: Settings > Advanced > Developer Mode
  ✓ Right-click server → Copy Server ID
### Empty Results from "Get channels list":
  Problem: Bot not in server or no channels visible
  ✓ Fix: Verify bot is invited to the server
  ✓ Check bot can see at least one channel
  ✓ Ensure limit is set high enough (default: 20)
### Merge Node Returns No Data:
  Problem: category_id mismatch between branches
  ✓ Fix: Check "Get Channels" has parent_id → category_id
  ✓ Verify "Get Server's Categories" has matching category_id

### Part 1: Create Bot & Get Token
  1. Go to Discord Developer Portal → Applications
  2. Click "New Application" → Name your bot
  3. Navigate to "Bot" tab → Click "Add Bot"
  4. Under Token section → Click "Reset Token"
  5. COPY THE TOKEN IMMEDIATELY (you won't see it again!)
  6. Keep this token secure - treat it like a password

### Part 2: Invite Bot to Your Server
  1. Go to OAuth2 → URL Generator
  2. Scopes section: ☑ bot
  3. Bot Permissions: 
     ☑ View Channels (required for v1.2)
     ☑ Read Message History (for future extensions)
     ☑ Send Messages (if you add messaging later)
  4. Copy the generated URL
  5. Paste in browser → Select your server → Authorize
  6. Verify bot appears in member list (may show as offline)

### Part 3: Add Credentials to n8n
  1. In n8n: Click "Credentials" (top-right) → Create New
  2. Search for "Discord Bot API" → Select it
  3. Credential Name: "Testing" (or match what's in workflow)
  4. Bot Token field: Paste the token from Part 1
  5. Click "Save" → Test connection if available
  6. Use this credential in all Discord nodes

## 🙏 Thank You for Trying This Workflow!
Your time and trust mean a lot! I truly appreciate you using this 
advanced Discord template.
### Your feedback shapes future updates:
- 💡 Suggestions for improvement
- 🆕 Ideas for new features  
- 📝 Requests for other automation workflows
#### Please share your thoughts! Every idea helps shape the next update.
### 🙋‍♂️ Join & Follow For More Free Templates!
- #### Discord Community: We Work Together
→ https://discord.gg/sEHaKcqBWY
Get help, share builds, collaborate!
- #### Facebook Fanpage: Automation Together
→ facebook.com/profile.php?id=61581574203648
  Daily tips, tutorials, and updates
- #### Youtube Channel: Automation Together
→ youtube.com/@AutomationTogether
Video guides for all templates
### Thank you again for being part of this journey! 🚀
### Together, we automate better! 🤖✨

### **Change Server ID:**
  • Update "Set - Server ID" node with different server
  • Bot must be invited to that server first
  • You can duplicate workflow for multiple servers

### **Advanced: Multi-Server Operations:**
  1. Replace "Set - Server ID" with "Code" node
  2. Create array: [server_id_1, server_id_2, ...]
  3. Add "Loop Over Items" before "Get channels list"
  4. Process multiple servers in one execution

### **Pro Tips:**
  ⚠️ Known Issue: "channe_id" has typo (missing 'l')
     • Keep it for compatibility with existing workflows
     • Or fix: Change in "Get Channels" node to "channel_id"
  
  💡 The parent_id logic is clever:
     • Categories have no parent (they ARE parents)
     • Text channels reference their category as parent
     • This is Discord's native structure

### **Extend "Next Step !" Placeholder:**
  Current workflow stops at data merging. You can add:
  
  💬 Send Messages to All Channels:
     • Add "Split In Batches" after Merge
     • Add "Discord: Send message" inside loop
     • Use ={{ $json.channe_id }} (note the typo!)
  
  📊 Export to Spreadsheet:
     • Add "Google Sheets" or "Excel" node
     • Map fields: channel_name, category_name, etc.
     • Great for server documentation
  
  🔍 Fetch Last Messages:
     • Add IF node: Check last_message_id not empty
     • Add "Discord: Get message" node
     • Use ={{ $json.last_message_id }}
  
  🎯 Category-Based Actions:
     • Add Switch node based on category_name
     • Route to different actions per category
     • Example: Announcements → Log, Support → Alert
